---
lang: en-EN
title: Supervised approach using TorchTextClassifiers

format:
  html:
    number-sections: true
---

This chapter introduces a baseline supervised classifier for text classification using PyTorch and TorchText. It is useful to compare with RAG results and check whether a simple traditional pipeline is enough for your task.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}

## Attention

- Use the same labeled dataset from `generation_None_temp08.parquet` to keep comparisons fair.
- Preprocess text consistently for training and inference (lowercase, tokenization, padding/truncation).
- Use GPU if available; otherwise set `device='cpu'` and smaller epochs for fast iteration.

:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="false" icon=false}
## `<i class="bi bi-book"></i>`{=html} Exercice 1: Prepare and train a TorchText classifier

1. Load dataset and split into train/validation/test.

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: Click to see the answer
#| code-overflow: scroll

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

annotations = pd.read_parquet('https://minio.lab.sspcloud.fr/projet-formation/diffusion/funathon/2026/project2/generation_None_temp08.parquet')
train_df, test_df = train_test_split(annotations, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)
train_df, val_df = train_test_split(train_df, test_size=0.1, random_state=42)
print('Train', len(train_df), 'Val', len(val_df), 'Test', len(test_df))
```

2. Define a TorchText Dataset and DataLoader, with simple tokenizer and vocab.

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: Click to see the answer
#| code-overflow: scroll

import torch
from torchtext.data.utils import get_tokenizer
from torchtext.vocab import build_vocab_from_iterator

tokenizer = get_tokenizer('basic_english')
def yield_tokens(data_iter):
    for text in data_iter:
        yield tokenizer(str(text))

vocab = build_vocab_from_iterator(yield_tokens(train_df['text']), specials=['<unk>'])
vocab.set_default_index(vocab['<unk>'])

text_pipeline = lambda x: vocab(tokenizer(str(x)))
label_pipeline = lambda x: int(x)

# Generate sample batch for sanity check
sample_input = text_pipeline(train_df['text'].iloc[0])
print('Sample', sample_input)
```

3. Train a small neural network and evaluate accuracy.

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: Click to see the answer
#| code-overflow: scroll

import torch.nn as nn

class SimpleTextClassifier(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, embed_dim, num_class):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(embed_dim, num_class)

    def forward(self, text_batch):
        embedded = self.embedding(text_batch).mean(dim=1)
        return self.fc(embedded)

model = SimpleTextClassifier(len(vocab), 128, annotations['label'].nunique())
print(model)
```

4. Add an evaluation clue.

<details>
<summary>Clue here</summary>
After training, compute `accuracy = correct / total` on validation and test sets and compare against RAG accuracy in the last notebook.
</details>

:::
